marketState: neutral-to-bullish medium-term, overbought and rich vs fair value short-term
summary: Front-month NG is trading at $3.245, about $0.32 (SLIGHTLY OVER) above the $2.929 modeled fair value, though that premium shrinks toward FAIR by +21 days as the seasonal fair-value curve rises to meet price. Medium-term drivers lean constructive: 8-16 day weather demand is running +18.7% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range, seasonality shows 63% odds of a higher price in 4 weeks with price already outperforming its median seasonal path, and managed money is net short but only at the 18th percentile of its 5-year range, a crowded position vulnerable to short-covering. Working against that is a bearish storage backdrop (+175 Bcf surplus vs 5y, this week's build beat forecast by +13 Bcf) and a weekly technical trend that is still bearish (50<200 EMA). Short-term, intraday technicals are extremely stretched (5m RSI 83.8, price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h) while 1-7 day weather demand has already cooled off 24h, arguing for a near-term pullback toward fair value before any resumption higher. Seasonality reliability is flagged Low (51pp spread), so conviction is capped on both horizons.
